content.text:
Looking at the image, the track runs straight ahead with the dashed centerline visible, and the car appears to be on track heading eastward with good alignment.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 112, target = (112 + 5) % 121 = 117
- Target WP117: x=5.6056, y=0.6843
- Car: x=2.1608, y=0.8227, heading=-10.93°
- dy = 0.6843 - 0.8227 = -0.1384
- dx = 5.6056 - 2.1608 = 3.4448
- bearing = atan2(-0.1384, 3.4448) = -2.30°
- delta = -2.30° - (-10.93°) = +8.63°
- Positive delta → waypoint is to the LEFT → small left steer

Delta has decreased from 19.2° to 8.63° — correction is working well. Now need only a small left correction.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track runs straight east ahead, car heading improved to -10.9° with delta=+8.6°, requiring only a small left correction to align with the centerline.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn (CCW heading increase), negative = right turn (CW)",
    "Track centerline y~0.68 at this straight section, car y~0.82 — slightly north of center",
    "Car heading correcting well: -43.96 → -25.60 → -21.19 → -10.93",
    "Delta decreasing well: 23.6 → 19.2 → 8.6 — correction nearly complete",
    "Track runs nearly due east for next ~20 waypoints (y stays ~0.68)",
    "Once delta < 5°, switch to 0 steering to avoid oscillation",
    "Track width 0.76m — very narrow, precision required",
    "Progress 94.85% — nearly complete lap, stay on track at all costs",
    "Oversteer warning: delta now <10°, use minimal steering (2.5 or 5) to avoid oscillation",
    "Car is left of center (y=0.82 vs centerline y=0.68), heading correction will bring it back"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [2.021, 2.062, 2.161],
    "y": [0.806, 0.808, 0.823],
    "yaw": [-22.43, -21.19, -10.93]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP117 at (5.606, 0.684): dy=-0.138, dx=3.445, bearing=-2.30°, delta=+8.6° → small LEFT steer needed",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 5.0
}
```
